frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: joint optimal encoding of natural-scene statistics under channel noise

frameworks.name: ON/OFF opponent pathways
frameworks.one_line: parallel detector grids signaling increments vs decrements across sensory space

core_question: How should grids of ON and OFF detectors be spatially arranged to optimally encode natural stimuli?
key_assumptions: sensory systems use parallel ON and OFF pathways encoding increments and decrements
encoding objective is joint efficient representation of natural-scene statistics
channel noise constrains the optimal encoder
manipulations: asymmetry of bright vs dark features in natural-scene statistics
relative spatial offset between ON and OFF mosaics
key_findings: optimal ON/OFF mosaic offset depends on interaction between bright/dark scene asymmetry and channel noise
empirical ON/OFF mosaic offsets across species are well predicted by the model
interpretations: relative arrangement of ON/OFF receptive field mosaics reflects efficient coding under noise
scene statistics and noise jointly determine cross-species mosaic geometry
